summary: Ukrainian FPV pilots are using tactics to disrupt Russian forces before striking, including attempts to dismount troops from horses. Russian forces have deployed the "Yolka" air defense drone for kinetic intercepts against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, and the "Rubikon" Center reported 104 targets destroyed on March 11, 2026, across multiple directions including Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. A Russian infantryman was killed in a bayonet charge against a Ukrainian FPV drone, and Russian forces destroyed a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle in Dobropole using a fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley." Ukrainian forces are fielding increasing numbers of "Molniya" strike and reconnaissance drones, which are difficult to counter due to unknown control frequencies. Ukrainian FPV drones have also struck Russian soldiers hiding behind trees with coordinated dual strikes.
